# Omar Al-Bakri — Applied AI Engineer

AI products fail at the handoff between commercial need and technical execution. Omar removes the handoff.

Omar Al-Bakri builds agent systems and full-stack AI products for financial services and other complex markets. Fifteen years in enterprise payments tells him where the problem is. Python and TypeScript let him carry it into production.

Bangkok-based · open to remote roles, consulting and contracting globally

## Remote roles

Senior or lead roles where product judgement, AI architecture and hands-on delivery need to sit with one person. Omar can own the product from the first problem statement to production.

## Consulting and contracting

Defined problems with a real delivery surface: AI products, agent workflows, production integrations, retrieval, evaluation, observability and failure handling. Not open-ended transformation theatre.

## Selected systems

### CallScore — Live product

Crypto commentary is easy to publish and hard to audit. CallScore extracts public creator market calls, matches them to timestamped price outcomes and scores evidence-backed performance.

### ruleIQ — Production-ready repository

Compliance does not fail because teams lack another chatbot. It fails when obligations, controls and evidence do not connect.

### Helios — Private build

Sales automation counts activity. Helios coordinates decisions through an operator surface, MCP services, graph memory and CRM integrations.

### thredOS — Pre-launch

Agent work disappears into prompts and terminal history. thredOS makes it explicit through dependencies, gates, approvals, runs and provenance.
